questions.optionA: A localized collection of blood outside blood vessels
questions.optionC: Fluid transudation in the pleural space
questions.optionD: Accumulation of fluid in the peritoneal cavity
questions.explanation: Anasarca is generalized massive edema of subcutaneous tissues.

questions.questionText: Which of the following promotes the formation of a transudate rather than an exudate?
questions.explanation: Transudates occur due to hydrostatic or oncotic pressure imbalances without increased permeability.

questions.questionText: Heart failure cells in the lungs are:
questions.explanation: Chronic passive congestion of the lungs leads to RBC extravasation, phagocytosed by macrophages.

questions.questionText: The lines of Zahn are characteristic finding in:
questions.explanation: Alternating pale and dark layers demonstrate thrombosis occurring in flowing blood.

questions.questionText: Virchow's triad includes all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Endothelial injury, abnormal blood flow (stasis), and hypercoagulability make up the triad.

questions.questionText: The most common source of pulmonary emboli is:
questions.optionC: Deep veins of the lower extremities
questions.explanation: More than 95% of pulmonary emboli arise from DVT above the knee.

questions.questionText: Paradoxical emboli characteristically pass through the:
questions.explanation: Venous emboli can enter the systemic circulation bypassing the lungs via an ASD/PFO.

questions.questionText: A wedge-shaped, pale (white) infarct is typically seen in the:
questions.explanation: Solid organs with end-arterial circulations (kidney, heart, spleen) develop pale infarcts.

questions.questionText: Red (hemorrhagic) infarcts typically occur in tissues with:
questions.explanation: Lungs and dual-circulation organs suffer red infarcts due to bleeding into the necrotic area.

questions.questionText: The primary pathophysiological mechanism in septic shock is:
questions.optionC: Massive systemic vasodilation and increased vascular permeability
questions.explanation: Endotoxins/Cytokines cause systemic vasodilation resulting in profound hypotension.

questions.questionText: In Hemodynamic Disorders, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Pathology texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Pathology references for Hemodynamic Disorders. Subject: Pathology. Chapter: Hemodynamic Disorders.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Hemodynamic Disorders scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Pathology. Chapter: Hemodynamic Disorders.
